column2:
Aviation Class
(as JCee)
column3:
(Sep 13, 1995) - Jordan Terrell Carter is born
(~2009) - JCee starts making music
(Sep 19, 2010) - JCee announces Aviation Class officially starting his music career
(???) - Aviation Class morphs into Killing Me Softly
(Apr 10, 2011) - JCee adapts the name "$ir Cartier"
column6: Carti would first starting working on music around 2009, with his first musical appearance being the "Jordan acting stupid" video, At the time, he was going by the name "JCee" (Abbreviation of Jordan and Carter, very creative Carti !), he revealed in an interview that his childhood nickname is "Moot". An old classmate of his revealed that he used to be at the after-class activities to make music in the school studio where the "Jordan acting stupid" video was filmed. He would first announce a mixtape called "Aviation class" on Sep 19, 2010, but didn't release anything, except a couple of singles 5 months later. On Apr 10, 2011 he would end up changing his name to $ir Cartier, and on Apr 27, 2011 he would announce a new mixtape called Killing Me Softly, which would not drop as well.

column2: [Unknown Major118 Collaborations]
column3: Carti was previously connected with a hip-hop collective named Major118, which included members Raiden, OddysseyMajor, Jovanni, and Kamari Esson as rappers, with Nneardark and SkaterVader as their producers. Multiple tweets dating back to 2013 indicate that Carti had ties to the group for a while. Given this, it's likely that they have multiple songs together, though the only known track at this time is "Flexin' ".

column2: [Unknown Cultie Marse Collaborations]
column3: Carti and Cultie used to be good friends back in the day and they very likely recorded a lot of songs together that we've never heard.

column2:
death in tune
(Awful Records, FACE, Red Tape)
column3:
(Jan 8, 2014) - Carti releases 'YUNGXANHOE' under the Artist Name 'Playboi Carti' on SoundCloud
(Early 2014) - Playboi Carti signs under the label 'AWFUL RECORDS'
(April 13, 2015) - Ian Connor announces 'Cash Carti - The Mixtape'.
(May 13, 2015) - Playboi Carti releases 'death in tune - EP'y
column6: In 2014, Playboi Carti signed to Awful Records and started gaining a lot of popularity and momentum. During this era Carti used a red cover for his SoundCloud singles, hence Red Tape also being a popular name for this period of Carti's career. The era ended with the release of death in tune EP, after which he changed his style and started working more with MexikoDro and generally different people than he worked with during the Awful Records Era.

column2:
Cash Carti The Mixtape
(Chucky Era, Cash Carti Season [V1])
column3:
(April 13, 2015) - Ian Connor mentions the Cash Carti Mixtape coming soon
(May 13, 2015) - Playboi Carti releases death in tune
(Jan 2, 2016) - Playboi Carti fails to release Mixtape & aesthetic and sound shifts
column6: After the release of death in tune, Playboi Carti's style shifted. He started collaborating more with MexikoDro, and going deeper into the Plugg sound. The era get's the name from the Chucky inspired covers for Carti's soundcloud singles during this era. The era ended on January 2, 2016 because Carti failed to release project & changed his sound and aesthetic again.

Ca$h Carti Season
(Cash Carti The Mixtape [V2])
column3:
(Jan 2016) - Playboi Carti's aesthetic and sound shifts
(Sep 9, 2016) - Playboi Carti signs to Interscope + AWGE and decides to rework his mixtape
column6: Carti changed his style in early 2016 after meeting many famous rappers. This was his third era, which he referred to as Ca$h Carti, and it was the largest embodiment of this era. Carti was at the peak of his flexing game and his career was moving at its fastest pace. This era concluded with him signing to AWGE and reworking the mixtape once again, which later released in 2017.

column2:
Playboi Carti
(Self Titled)
column3:
(September 9, 2016) - Playboi Carti signs to Interscope + AWGE
(April 14, 2017) - Playboi Carti is officially released
column6: After his signing to AWGE and Interscope records, Carti's next mixtape began to change focus, and the mixtape created with Awful Records was transformed. Playboi Carti is the self-titled third mixtape from Playboi Carti, executively produced by A$AP Rocky, with heavy production from Pi'erre Bourne. The album would heavily focus on repetitive earworm performances over Pi'erre's signature psychedelic trap beats. It was supported by three singles, "Lookin", "wokeuplikethis*", and "Magnolia", the latter of which was awarded Best New Track from Pitchfork.

column2:
16*29
(Collaboration with Lil Uzi Vert)
column3:
(Mid 2017) - The project is concieved
(January 3rd & 4th, 2018) - Major sessions for the project occur
(May 11, 2018) - Playboi Carti releases "Die Lit"
(June 19th, 2018) - DP Beats releases "Bankroll" to his streaming pages
(July 30th, 2018) - Carti states in a radio show interview that him and uzi have like 'over 100 songs' since 2015
(September 25th, 2018) - Chinatown releases Squad before it gets taken down
(September 2018) - 16*29 doesn't release and the project is scrapped
column6:
In September of 2017, Playboi Carti announced via Snapchat and Twitter that he would be collaborating with Lil Uzi Vert on a joint album titled 1629*. The following month, a tour for the album was announced by the two, but it was shortly canceled afterward when Uzi said he would not be going on tour with Carti because he "needed to focus." The album would fall by the wayside until July of 2018, when Carti mentioned that he and Uzi had recorded "like 100 songs." After that, all talk of the album pretty much subsided. It should be noted that many insiders have claimed that there was never a tracklist, cover art, or marketing plan put together.

The green names can't be used because of the color scheme so they'll be replaced with the yellow ones.

column2: Die Lit
column3:
(April 14, 2017) - Playboi Carti releases "Playboi Carti"
(May 11, 2018) - Playboi Carti releases "Die Lit"
column6:
Die Lit is Playboi Carti’s debut studio album and a follow-up to his eponymous debut mixtape. The album is executively produced by Pi'erre Bourne and boasts features from Young Thug, Lil Uzi Vert, Travis Scott, Nicki Minaj, and Skepta, among many others.

Die Lit is a reminder of the power of simplicity, as the album is full of effortless vibes and hypnotic flows that create an array of catchy, uncomplicated tracks. Carti explores different vocal inflections, and he emphasizes his Atlanta accent in his delivery.

Before the album’s release, fifteen songs recorded during production of the album were leaked online. However, only four of the leaked tracks were actually on the album; these are “Shoota” (the leaked version was a rough demo), “Love Hurts,” “Choppa Won’t Miss,” and “Foreign.”

On May 2, 2018, Carti officially released “Love Hurts” on SoundCloud as the album’s lead single.

column2:
Whole Lotta Red [V1]
(Die Lit 2)
column3:
(May 11, 2018) - Die Lit is officially released 
(June 3rd, 2018) - While previewing Buffy The Body, Carti says that Die Lit 2 is coming soon
(Aug 9, 2018) - Carti makes Skeleton, where he mentions Whole Lotta Red, making the name change from 'Die Lit 2' to 'Whole Lotta Red' official
(July 29, 2019) - Carti claims that he will try to drop in the next 60 days [1]
(October 2019) - Carti fails to drop in the next 60 days
column6: The first iteration of Whole Lotta Red aimed for a very similar sound to Die Lit, with Carti rapping over cloudy trap beats, and producer Pi'erre Bourne reprising his role as the premier beatmaker for the album with one difference: his now-famous "Baby Voice," prominent on songs like "Pissy Pamper" and "Goku." Around a month after the release of Die Lit, Carti previewed "Buffy The Body" on a private account Instagram Live and announced he was working on Die Lit 2. In August of 2018, a video surfaced of Carti discussing the album, now referring to it as Whole Lotta Red.

column2: Die Lit 2
column3:
(May 11, 2018) - Die Lit is officially released
(June 3rd, 2018) - While previewing Buffie The Body, Carti says that Die Lit 2 is coming soon
(Aug 2018) - Carti mentions 'Whole Lotta Red' in an interview

One of the biggest grails in the Carti community. Richie leaked the beat in the Richie Grail zip, confirming the name as "Tryna Get Down". The voice memo is dated April 13, 2019, so it's most likely from early April, though the original beat was made on February 9, 2019, meaning it could've been made anytime between February 9 and April 13. Technically apart of V1 due to being recorded before failing to "drop in the next 60 days".

Contrary to popular belief, this song is not lost as Richie Souf has talked to people who own the song but will likely not surface for a very long time.

Richie ended up dropping the beat as part of his Carti beat pack a few years later.

column2:
Whole Lotta Red [V2]
(Blonde Era, Baby Voice Era, Opium Era)
column3:
(Sep 30, 2019) - Carti fails to drop in "the next 60 days")
(Oct 16, 2019) - Landmark session happens
(April 16, 2020) - @ MEH releases and receives poor reception
(June 28, 2020) - BigSosa, Carti's long-time friend passes sending Carti into a hiatus and causing a change of sound
column6:
The second iteration of Whole Lotta Red represents Carti's peak of experimentation, pushing his "baby voice" and high-pitched vocals to new extremes. He collaborated with Pi’erre Bourne, Richie Souf, and Maaly Raw, floating over a mix of heavenly synth beats and hard trap, but after mixed reactions to the V2 sound upon the release of "@ MEH" (everbody thought it was fucking shit), Carti scrapped and reworked the project before its final release. The cover art from this era comes from the @ MEH single, marking a transitional phase.

Carti also underwent a major aesthetic transformation, dyeing his hair blonde, switching from freeform dreads to re-twists, and embracing avant-garde fashion, especially Rick Owens. The upside-down cross became central to his imagery, linking him to his Opium collective. Though initially divisive, the V2 sound is now regarded as a fan-favorite, with many tracks considered grails, marking this as one of his most experimental and influential phases.

"U Kan Do It Too" is an unreleased throwaway from "Whole Lotta Red", which was intended to be the lead single before being scrapped for "@ MEH". The file was mixed and mastered for release, aswell as having the paperwork and ASCAP filed. Submitted for copyright on October 10, 2019. Listed on DSPs as "u can do it too." on October 25, 2019, with the cover art being just a black square. It's unknown if this was meant to be the cover or just a placeholder. The song was recorded before October 2019, meaning that it might've been recorded during the 2019 V1 sessions; it was meant to release as the lead single for the reworked version of the album.

column2:
Whole Lotta Red [V3]
(Pre-Kanye)
column3:
(Apr 14, 2020) - @ MEH releases to poor reception from fans
(June 28th, 2020) - BigSosa, Carti's long-time friend passes away sending Carti into a hiatus?*
(Summer 2020) - Carti starts exploring a 'darker' sound
(November 2020) - Ye becomes executive producer and the album sound shifts
column6: After the death of his friend Bigg Sosa, Carti takes more time to release the album and abandons social media until October, where he finally made his return to announce the rollout of his awaited second studio album Whole Lotta Red. We know that Carti isn't a really good lyricist, but on certain tracks, he talks about the grief he experienced when Bigg Sosa passed and expresses how he copes on songs like 'ILoveUIHateU,' where he opens up about his drug addiction. Songs during this era have more of a somber, relaxed tone compared to the rest of his works, abandoning his baby-voice for an early version of what we know as his raspy voice. This shift was partly driven by the immense hate and criticism he received for the baby-voice, pushing him to explore a darker and more aggressive vocal style. Leakers say that there was a September version of Whole Lotta Red with a finalized tracklist, but we will probably never hear what this version of the album sounded like.

column2:
Whole Lotta Red [V4]
(exec. prod. Kanye West)
column3:
(November 2020) - Ye becomes executive producer and the album sound shifts
(December 25, 2020) - Whole Lotta Red is officially released
column6: In late 2020, Carti would shift the sound of Whole Lotta Red into being an aggressive ode to punk rock aesthetics, while maintaining the repetitive vocal style that made his previous work so loved. The album was his biggest musical departure by far, forgoing most of the things that brought him success in the first place, such as production from Pi'erre Bourne, or features from popular collaborators like Lil Uzi Vert or A$AP Rocky. Instead, the album would only see a handful of features, and would see production predominantly from F1LTHY & Art Dealer. Connecting to V3, on this album Carti talks about his acts violence, expressing how he felt when Bigg Sosa died (grief to anger). On songs like On That Time, Stop Breathing and No Sl33p, he opens up on how he felt, displaying anger, sometimes sadness and vengence for his friend. Ye would be the executive producer for the album, and it would see a release on Christmas Day, 2020.

column2: Whole Lotta Red (Deluxe)
column3:
(December 25, 2020) - Whole Lotta Red is officially released
(December 26, 2020) - Carti asks his fans on twitter what songs they want on the Deluxe.
(March 10th, 2021) Carti says that he wants to drop a new album and drop rn
(Early 2021) - Carti states that the deluxe will come in Spring 2021
(Spring 2021) - Deluxe is shelved
column6: One day after Whole Lotta Red's release, Carti took to Twitter to ask fans which songs they wanted on the deluxe edition, marking the first time he had ever planned a deluxe release for one of his projects. Many of the songs considered for the deluxe were well-known leaks, alongside potential new tracks created specifically for the project. These songs underwent multiple tweaks and changes, such as mixing, mastering, and even name changes, in preparation for an official release. So far, two tracklist versions of the deluxe have been revealed by insiders and leakers, with speculation that more versions may exist but have yet to surface. Unfortunately, Carti eventually went silent about the deluxe, and the project was ultimately shelved. It is rumored that the label did not allow him to release the deluxe, forcing him to abandon the project in favor of focusing on his next album, NARCISSIST.

column3: (December 25, 2020 - March, 2021) - Carti continues to record music without a clear idea on what to aim for

(Balaclava Era, DONDA: WITH CHILD, DND WTH CHLD, DND, God's Country, The First Born Donda)
column3:
(Dec 2020) - Ye & Playboi Carti start working together on Donda
(Aug 29, 2021) - Donda is officially released on all streaming services
column6: Donda was an opportunity used by Carti as a "rollout" to showcase his new, somewhat softer, and more relaxed sound used on his now-scrapped album "Narcissist". Based on snippets and leaks, Ye West not only helped Carti develop a foundation for the album, but he also gave inspiration and introduced him to the sounds he uses today. Despite the non-launch of Narcissist, Carti used Donda as a stepping stone to his new works of music.

column2: Narcissist
column3:
(Early 2021) - Whole Lotta Red (Deluxe) gets shelved
(March 10, 2021) - Carti posts about wanting to drop soon
(July 7, 2021) - Carti goes to the Mercedez Benz Stadium, to work on Donda
(August 23, 2021) - Carti posts fit pic on IG captioned: "NARCISSIST 09/13/21"
(September 13, 2021) - NARCISSIST does not get released 
(Mar 2, 2022) - First known Guapo voice song is recorded
column6: After shelving the WLR Deluxe, Carti entered his most mysterious era. He announced Narcissist for September 13, 2021, but it never dropped due to sample clearance issues and its similarities to Future’s sound. Fans speculated the album might be called Mollyworld after a fit pic caption, but Narcissist was the final name. The project hinted at a futuristic, sample-heavy sound, blending Carti’s fading “baby voice” with a developing deeper tone — a shift later heard on Travis Scott’s “FE!N.” Leaks like “Ready to Crash” and “Too Hot” showcased his evolving style. Despite a producer lineup that included Richie Souf, F1lthy, and Art Dealer, the album was scrapped, and the tour was renamed King Vamp. The only official release was a controversial merch line. Despite multiple sources claiming it's not an album, Carti posted a screenshot revealing the existence of this album.

column3: Full

(feat. Vory) (prod. Digital Nas)
(24 Songs, We Made It This Far, Made It)
column3: Before leaking, one of the holy grails of Carti Comm. Previewed on Digital Nas's Stream during the 2021 Donda Sessions in the Mercedes-Benz Stadium. Confirmed to be titled "Made It This Far" by Jett. Privately purchased and leaked for free by Hells on March 23, 2023 (Filename is fake). Adin Ross said that Carti was furious when Digital Nas filmed the snippet. Samples "Analog" by Prepare My Glider. Later in 2025, Carti would play this multiple times during his Antagonist 2.0 Tour (very likely a version made by yeditors Lionel Scott, Akimbo, and Louie Bag as well)

It was mistakenly believed that the track's name was "Two Out" but Carti was actually saying "Too Hot", though everyone knows it as "Two Out", the real name is unknown. A session for the song was up for bonus for the "Melrose" GB, but got removed after the price dropped. Rumored to have been on an early 'Narcissist' draft.

column2:
⭐ Sights
(with A$AP Rocky) (prod. evilgiane & Babyxsosa)
(OUR DE$TINY)
column3:
OG Filename: pbc x flacko 5.2.21 2
On September 2, 2022, A$AP Rocky uploaded the song to his Instagram along with it's music video. On September 7, 2022, controversy started when opiumbaby called out and dissed Rocky for "leaking the song", saying that Carti didn’t approve the track's release and that it was Carti's song, not Rocky's. Fritz Owens later confirmed this. OGF with an open verse would end up leaking on September 9, 2022. Rocky later performed the song at Rolling Loud New York on September 25, 2022. Much later in 2023, when Rocky was asked about the song in an interview, he explained that he posted it because "They just kept leaking it, so we just said 'fuck it' and put it out", despite the fact that the song was completely unheard up until the moment Rocky leaked it himself. The track samples "If" by Destiny's Child and "Dirty White Speaks / Make Em Say Huh" by Rick Ross.

column2: MUSIC [V1]
column3:
(Sep 13, 2021) - Playboi Carti fails to release 'Narcissist'.
(Mar 10, 2022) - Playboi Carti reveals the album is now called 'MUSIC' in a interview with XXL,
(Dec 24, 2022) - Playboi Carti ends his social media hiatus by posting what is believed to be the album cover on Instagram.
(Dec 29, 2022) - Playboi Carti gets arrested on domestic violence charges, forcing Carti to push back the album from an alleged Jan 13, 2023 release date.
(Late May 2023) - Playboi Carti starts to shift towards an industrial, dark, and expeimental sound for the album.
column6: In early 2023, anticipation for Playboi Carti’s next album was through the roof. He had just previewed “ROCKSTARZ” at Rolling Loud California, and it felt like the album could drop any week. Around this time, DJ Swamp Izzo emerged as a trusted insider in the Carti community, sharing key info in DMs with a fan. He claimed Carti had a few possible titles in mind, despite previously teasing the name MUSIC in 2022, and said the official title would drop in two weeks—which never happened. This led to speculation about whether this was a new project, or just MUSIC under a different name. Swamp also mentioned that about 25 tracks were in consideration and hinted at an April or May release. Swamp stated that he would speak over each song—something that later became a main gimmick of MUSIC when it released—and that the sound for the tape was inspired by Lil Wayne, with Carti blending all of his sounds together with a touch of 2000s Atlanta trap. After two grueling years of constant blueballing, Carti finally dropped MUSIC, and once it was out, more details surfaced about the behind-the-scenes process. In an April 2025 interview, Swamp Izzo revealed Carti had completed an entire tape by the end of 2022—but no tracks from it made the final tracklist for MUSIC.

OG Filename: pbc 8-15-22 2 roark
Snippet leaked on August 13, 2024. It was supposed to be groupbought instead of "HOMICIDE55555"; the server owners ran the buy believing this was the song to be acquired. It was ultimately an elaborate scam that the middleman (tune) perpetrated with the seller so that they could sell a less desirable song for a higher profit. Further, when it leaked the outro was cut and Soul spread a rumor about the cut portion of the song including a beat switch. The seller also lied about the song having Clams Casino production, when the real producer was Roark Bailey. 

Samples "It's Ok, You're Ok" by Bonjr, which itself samples "Seven Forty Seven" by Boards Of Canada. Leaked on Aug 4, 2025. OG file leaked via Soul on April 24, 2026, but this might still be cut.

column2:
004KT
(Collaboration with YoungBoy Never Broke Again)
column3:
(~2022) - The project is conceived
(June 2023) - Carti posts a picture of YoungBoy wearing an opium chain on his story
(April 16, 2024) - YoungBoys home is raided and he is arrested, later indicted and convicted of running a large scale drug ring. Project likely scrapped around this time
(March 24, 2025) - YoungBoy is released from federal prison and Carti posts a picture of YoungBoy wearing a opium chain on his story again
(May 20, 2025) - Carti reposts YB again
(May 21st, 2025) - ALIVE is released
column6: 004KT, stylized a 004KTWHOYOUHATE?, is a (likely scrapped) collaboration project between Playboi Carti and NBA Youngboy, first teased in February 2023 when DJ Swamp Izzo claimed that 'YoungBoys tape with Carti is almost done'. Then, in the summer of 2023, Carti gifted YoungBoy an opium chain and then posted a picture of YoungBoy wearing the chain. F1LTHY also tweeted the words '004KT' (00 meaning Opium and 4KT standing for YoungBoys label) hinting at his potetional involvement in a collaboration between the two. The album was in the works up until early 2024, when YoungBoy was raided and arrested for running a large scale drug ring.

column2:
MUSIC [V2]
(Cave Era)
column3:
(Jan 13, 2023) - Playboi Carti fumbles the alleged album release date, scrapping the copy and concept of the album planned.
(Late May 2023) - Carti starts to shift towards an industrial, dark and expeimental sound for the album.
(June 2023) - Carti tours across Europe, temporarily resides in Paris for the cave sessions where he records more 'experimental' music.
(August 2023) - Carti leaves the cave causing a switch of sound.
(August 31, 2023) - Antagonist Tour get's postponed.
(Sep 1, 2023) - Carti plans to release WICKED as the lead single alongside a Music Video 3 weeks after the plan was conceived. The same day the song gets leaked online causing Carti to scrap it.
column6:
After the rollout for the 'GUAPO' era of MUSIC died down, Carti would go back into a hiatus, however, this wouldn't last for long, as Carti was booked for multiple festivals across Europe in the summer of 2023. And during this time of being in Europe, Carti would rent out a cave in Paris, and record various songs there. The songs recorded here were more experimental, and not like the rest of his music. Songs like POP OUT, MEET YO MAKER, and DOCTOR were all recorded in these caves, and most of the songs recorded here were produced by either Pi'erre Bourne or F1LTHY. Carti would eventually end up going back on this cave sound and exploring a darker theme, the only songs from this era (that we know of atleast) seeing an official release being POP OUT and RADAR.

The cover for this era is actually a fake cover after the creator behind the cover lied about "cartis team considering it", when in reality it was only an auto-reply email. The cover was originally posted on IG as a fan art. Inspired by “Eaten Back To Life” by Cannibal Corpse.

column3:
A grail since its first snippet surfaced. It was initially believed by many to be an AI/Fake track. The real file has only 1:20 minutes of vocals and an open verse. This file has vocals looped over the open verse instead, making this a Self-Bounce, as it was modified from it's original state by Shogani. The sessions for this song are in the hands of leakers who claim that the song was never finished. 

For some time, there was a fake filename that claimed that this track was made on May of 2022, which was completely illogical since both artists had a way different flow back then. This got supported by @uzirareupdates revealing the real recording dates; with Carti recording his only part on May of 2023 (alongside 'FOMDJ'), and Uzi one day before Pink Tape released. There's a chance that this track was another scrapped feature from Carti for Uzi's album, which obviously didn't make the cut.

Drake - K-9 [V3]
(with Playboi Carti) (prod. Scott Bridgeway, Nasamadeit, F1LTHY & Glasear) 
(SLIDE IN, READY OR NOT)
column3:
OG Filename: K-9 NOEL REF
Drake's part was first previewed by Swamp Izzo in a club on June 25, 2025; albeit an updated version. It was then confirmed to have a beat switch to Carti's part when another snippet surfaced on Oct 15, 2025 by cold. Adin Ross would then play another snippet of Drake's verse on KICK on November 10, 2025.  

It was originally considered for a release on Drake's 2023 "For All The Dogs" before being scrapped for unknown reasons. Was also considered for a release on the March 14th, 2025 version of "MUSIC" (via DJ Akademiks) but was most likely scrapped due to Kendrick Lamar's influence on the album. 

The instrumental of Drake's part comes from a Nasamadeit beat uploaded to YouTube, which samples "A House Is Not A Home" by Luther Vandross. [1] 
In fact, this track is basically a stem edit made by Drake's team, combining his song "K-9" with Carti's track "READY OR NOT". Carti likely gave the song to Drake when he returned to America.

CDQ leaked for free on November 22, 2025 after it had been private bought. Shortly after surfacing, F1LTHY validated the concept of "the Cave" by commenting that it was actually recorded in the infamous cave in France. [2].

column2: CARTI YE
column3:
(October 2023) Carti starts working with Ye on Vultures in Italy
(March 15th, 2025) Ye confirms that the collab album exists and got scrapped because of the beef between them
column6: CARTI YE is a collaborative project between Playboi Carti and Kanye West. Little is known of the album, except that sessions for the project took place and it was ultimately scrapped. Kanye posted the album cover on X on March 15, 2025, alongside confirming that the project was discontinued. Later on December 11, 2025, on a stream with Cuffem, F1LTHY would come out months later (Dec 11, 2025) and claim that it was actually a track meant for their scrapped collaborative album "CARTI YE". So Ye uploading it without Carti was part of the episode he was having at the time, starting a beef with Carti. YoungBoy for his part, decided to stick to Carti's side. Which would bring back the 004KT duo from 2022-2023 to the public, generating more anticipation for their future tracks together. He actually had the song for himself in the start, before the other two added their own contributions.

column2:
MUSIC [V3]
(I AM MUSIC)
column3:
(October 2023) - Carti heads over to Italy to record for VULTURES
(Nov 2023) - The 'MUSIC' sessions begin
(Jan 5, 2024) - MUSIC supposedly is meant to drop, it does not
(Mar 27, 2024) - Carti stays quiet after his last feature on the intial rollout.
(Dec 15, 2024) - A partial LP for the album is held at Rolling Loud, with another failed launch date between Dec 25 and January 2025
(March 14th, 2025) - MUSIC officially releases on all platforms
column6:
Playboi Carti's third studio album, MUSIC, was released on March 14, 2025. After a failed rollout in January 2023, Carti revamped the album concept, embracing a darker, gothic, and metal-inspired musical aesthetic. This new sound was driven by gritty beats, mainly produced by Cardo Got Wings, and a mix of deep, menacing vocal delivery with his signature whispery, melodic style.

In 2023, Carti largely went silent, resurfacing in July with a feature on Travis Scott's UTOPIA on the track "FE!N," which showcased his newly adopted deep vocal tone. In December 2023, he dropped the first single, "DIFFERENT DAY," followed by "2024," which highlighted both his vocal range and a shift toward sample-based production. Singles like "HBA," "BACKR00MS" with Travis Scott, "EVIL J0RDAN," and "KETAMINE" continued to generate anticipation. He also featured on major projects, including VULTURES 1 and We Don't Trust You, maintaining his presence in the spotlight. The album's release in March 2025 was highly anticipated, marking a significant evolution in Carti's sound. This was also the first time Carti has incorporated AI into his music, using Keith Lawson to record songs for him and run it through a filter to turn it into his voice. Songs like RATHER LIE and FINE SHIT are very clearly AI tracks.

Unfortunately, this track began as a reference track done by Keith Lawson, which was then ran by AI filters for both Justin Bieber and Carti. However, only Carti ended up actually recording for the track. His team actually sent this file to Justin Bieber. It's rumored that they asked him if they could keep his AI chorus, because the track was literally "finished" one day before the album was released. Part of the rumor tells that JB rejected that idea, which in turn resulted in the track being scrapped at the last minute. All of this is backed up by Carti heavily teasing Justin's feature on the album around this time.

Song was bounced by holder or seller, the song was never bounced by the engineer, it was sourced as a session with no bounce similiar to other sessions such as Athena, Dreams/Selena, Medusa, YSL Cheetah etc. OG Filename is false, recording date is possibly real

column2:
VULTURES
(Bad Bitch Playbook, ¥$)
column3:
(Mid 2023) - The project is concieved 
(October 2023) - Carti starts working with Ye on Vultures in Italy
(Feb 10, 2024) - Vultures 1 is officially released on all streaming services
(Aug 3, 2024) - Vultures 2 is officially released on all streaming services
(September 2024) - Ye shifts his focus to BULLY
column6: Carti was a frequent collaborator, for Ye and Ty Dolla $ign's joint trilogy (cut by two volumes), Vultures. 'MUSIC' was even delayed, because of the delays and reworks these albums had. During this sub-era, Carti would do some changes to his own project, due to Ye's influence. Eventhough only a very few songs were released or leaked, it's believed that 'the Vultures' and Carti may have worked on so much more material together.

column2:
BABY BOI
(ANTAGONIST 2.0, BBYBOI)
column3:
(January 2023) - The concept of 2 albums dropping back to back is conceived.
(March 14, 2025) - MUSIC releases on all DSPs.
(March 15, 2025) - Carti posts a screenshot of the Apple Music charts with the caption "BABY BOI OTW" on Instagram.
(May 21, 2025) - ALIVE & SOME MORE release on Instagram.
(September 2025) - BABY BOI is supposed to release before the Antagonist 2.0 Tour starts but doesn't due to clearances issues.
(Ongoing)
column6: In 2023, Carti would confirm in a facetime call that he planned to drop 2 albums; MUSIC and BABY BOI. The plans for BABY BOI are unknown, however, Carti has alluded to it multiple times after the release of MUSIC, claiming it will come soon. Insiders have also said the album will drop before the end of 2025, with Carti consistently reitterating that it will drop before the end of 2025 aswell. It was planned to drop but was cancelled.

column3: Full

(prod. DJ Moon & Lucian)

OG Filename: Carti 1.8.26 song 1
Allegedly the first finished song Carti recorded in 2026. It's also claimed to be untitled. First snippet was previewed by Raahim on January 13, 2026. Second snippet was previewed by Cold on January 14, 2026. Third snippet was previewed by Jamal and right after Raahim leaked the extended version of the snippet on January 24, 2026. A new snippet was previewed by Cold on January 31, 2026. The full song would leak after a successful GB on March 2, 2026.

[2] Six days later, after being asked to play a snippet of the Carti song, he would say that he doesn't have it, but that it was a remix of his and Percaso's song 'Brazy Brazy'. [3]

column2: [Unknown February 2024 Album Copy]
column3: According to Heavenorhell, back when MUSIC was considered for a January 2024 release, BABY BOI was set to follow in February 2024. This can seemingly be verified by the FaceTime call with A$AP Bari sometime in January 2023 [1] as Playboi Carti said he wanted to "drop two of 'em, back to back, February." This idea was ulitmately scrapped however after Ye made Carti delay MUSIC for the then-planned VULTURES trilogy.

column2: [Unknown Pre-October 2025 Album Copy]
column3: According to Pop, a copy of the album was release ready before the ANTAGONIST 2.0 Tour but was scrapped due to clearance issues.

column3: Carti and DJ Swamp Izzo have been pictured in the studio together. Izzo later went onto VIS Unfiltered to announce a finished collaboration tape between him and Carti dropping in summer 2023.

column2: [Unknown Destroy Lonely Collaborations]

Destroy Lonely confirmed on his interview with Complex, that he and Carti have recorded tons of songs together throughout the years. 
Destroy Lonely has quoted: "me and Carti make just as much music as me and Ken do”. He also implied that they recorded tons of songs throughout 2025 aswell.

It's possible that he did collaborate with some of them as Earl and Carti previously worked together to release "2024".

column2: [Unknown Justin Bieber Collaborations]

column2: [Unknown Ken Carson Collaborations]
column3: Carti would post fit pics with Ken Carson and Rocky in the studio. The songs they recorded were either for BABY BOI, More Chaos, or DON'T BE DUMB.

column2: [Unknown Lil Baby Collaborations]
column3: Pictures of Lil Baby & Playboi Carti in the studio surfaced on Jan 10th, 2026. They may also have more songs from 2025 that we haven't heard.

column2: [Unknown Lil Yachty Collaborations]
column3: Playboi Carti & Lil Yachty were seen together on Feb 16, 2026. It is possible that they have made some songs together.

column2: [Unknown LilYoGang Collaborations]
column3: LilYoGang was with Carti during the Antagonist Tour 2.0, Carti also recently liked a post from On The Radar on instagram of Ealuhri doing a freestyle performance, so they may have made music together.

column2: [Unknown Lil Uzi Vert Collaborations]
column3: Many people were speculating and getting hyped off the idea that Uzi would be a guest at Playboi Carti's 'ANTAGONIST' show, in Philly (November 10, 2025). However, this did not happen. Meek Mill turned out to be the special guest, despite Uzi's team being present at the venue. And yet, Carti did shoutout Uzi ("16*29 FOREVER"). He even stated: "I just saw my best friend Uzi", while also shouting out Maaly Raw. Even though there's a chance the two have finally got back to work together, this can also be very unlikely.

column2: [Unknown MC Lan Collaborations]
column3: 2nd album is said to have pop sound, so it's likely that MC Lan had some input on this album. Also according to Heavenandhell Carti worked with him.

column2: [Unknown Offset Collaborations]
column3: Offset confirmed that he has new songs with Carti.

column2: [Unknown Pz' Collaborations]
column3: Pz' has been actively present around Playboi Carti since June 2025, so it is very likely that they've recorded music together.

column2: [Unknown Pi'erre Bourne Collaborations]
column3: While playing Fortnite with fans, Carti said that he was in the studio working with Pi'erre a few days ago.

column2: [Unknown PlaqueBoyMax Collaborations]
column3: On Twitter/X, Jamal revealed that PlaqueBoyMax was in the studio with Carti at one point. And also has many songs with Ken Carson & Destroy Lonely. Ken would later respond to the tweet saying to "not trust him".

column2: [Unknown Rod Wave Collaborations]
column3: Carti posted Rod Wave on his story with the caption "OMERTÀ", indicating that he likely made some songs with him. He was also at Carti's club party at Palo Santo in Atlanta on April 1, 2025.

column2: [Unknown Skaiwater Collaborations]
column3: Footage of him, Carti, and Ken Carson has surfaced.

column2: [Unknown Summer Walker Collaborations]
column3: Summer Walker posted a pic on her story of Carti in the studio and thanked him "for pulling up to add a great finishing touch to my album" meaning that he had recorded a possible feature (that didn't drop).

column2: [Unknown The Weeknd Collaborations]
column3: The Weeknd and Carti have recorded multiple songs together according to members of the community, it is unknown what else was recorded besides "Popular", "Timeless" and "RATHER LIE". Additionally, they could have been recording new music whilst they were linked up during the After Hours Til Dawn tour.

column2: [Unknown Travis Scott Collaborations]
column3: Carti is said to have recorded many songs with Travis during this era. It was also said that Carti was working with Ye after the ANTAGONIST 2.0 Tour for his collab album with Travis Scott.

column2: [Unknown Trippie Redd Collaborations]
column3: It is possible that Playboi Carti & Trippie Redd have made some songs together when they met in Miami in 2025.

column2: [Unknown Ye Collaborations]
column3: Ye is said to have been working with Playboi Carti post-ANTAGONIST 2.0 Tour, potentially for Ye's collab album with Travis Scott.

column2: [Unknown YoungBoy Never Broke Again Collaborations]
column3: Rumors of Playboi Carti and NBA Youngboy collaborating started in June 2023, after Carti had allegedly gifted Youngboy a chain ahead of their "collab album".

Tracker Guidelines
1. Put music videos, and everything unreleased that isn't a song, art, stems, or a tracklist, in misc.
2. When adding a new era (or recoloring/renaming the era), use conditional formatting (right-click → View more cell actions → Conditional formatting). Otherwise, it won't work on the TrackerHub app.

3. To edit anything in Best Of, Special, Grails, Wanted, Worst Of, AI Ref Tracks, Recent, Recently Recorded, or OG Files, edit it in Unreleased. Those sections work as filters, filtering through the Unreleased section by adding all songs that meet the corresponding emoji or criteria, such as Leak Date, File Date, or the OG File having an Available Length.
4. Use imgur.gg for all links, because KrakenFiles links are deleted after some time. If something can't be uploaded to imgur.gg due to large file size, then use kraken or Mega.
5. Check if the song/mv/whatever is already added before you add it.
If you got any problems with the tracker or would like something changed you can join https://discord.gg/2900 and create a thread in #tracker-suggestions
